scenario: Beacon Logistics dispatches 1200 tasks per day through Camunda 8.8 Tasklist. A new agent sees three date columns on each task tile and is unsure whether the due date or the follow up date marks the deadline for finishing the task, versus the latest moment at which she should start working on it, so her team lead asks for a precise mapping.
question: Which statement correctly distinguishes the follow up date and the due date on a Tasklist task in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: The follow up date marks when an automatic escalation message fires, while the due date marks when the broker times out the task with an incident
The follow up date is the deadline for finishing the task, while the due date marks the latest moment the assignee should start working on the task
The follow up date is a reminder timestamp set by the assignee, while the due date is a server clock that ticks down once the task has been claimed
The follow up date marks the latest moment the assignee should start work, while the due date marks the deadline by which the task should be finished

a.text: Incorrect. Neither field triggers automatic escalation messages or broker incidents in Tasklist. The user guide says the follow up date defines when work should start and the due date defines the finish deadline.

b.text: Incorrect. The two roles are reversed in this option. The follow up date defines when work should start and the due date defines the deadline by which the task should be finished.

c.text: Incorrect. The follow up date and due date are process data attached to the user task, not assignee-set reminders or claim-driven server timers. The mapping is start versus finish.

d.text: Correct. The Ordering section states that the follow up date defines the latest time you should start working on a task and that the due date provides a deadline for when the task should be finished.

explanation: Tasklist distinguishes the follow up date (the latest moment the assignee should start work on a task) from the due date (the deadline by which the task should be finished). Both dates are available as ordering keys alongside creation date and priority.
